Putting the whole scoring engine at top-level eval/ (no gdos/ subdir) was rejected: it discards the useful purity guarantee that makes the gates trivially testable from hand-built bundles. Splitting on 'is it P5' rather than 'does it execute' was rejected: it is the property that matters (determinism/replay + testability), not the phase label.

decisions.title: Portal scope narrowed to Poki only; CrazyGames removed; toolchain bumped to latest-as-of-implementation packages
decisions.why_it_exists: User explicitly narrowed REQ-001/REQ-170 (previously Poki + CrazyGames) to Poki only, and directed that any package used in the project run the latest version published as of the current implementation date (2026-07), not a stale pin.
decisions.problem_it_solves: Prevents scoping P9/P11 SDK-integration work (S9.5) around two portal SDKs when only one is currently targeted, avoiding a build/test surface for a CrazyGames SDK contract that is not needed. Separately, keeps the toolchain from silently drifting behind the latest stable npm releases over a multi-month project.
decisions.emotion_targeted: N/A (scope/tooling decision, not a player-facing mechanic).
decisions.misconception_created: CrazyGames is removed from the backlog/IRD/task_slices entirely, not marked deferred — a future session must not assume CrazyGames support exists or is planned; re-adding it means re-deriving REQ-001/REQ-170 wording and a new S9.x slice from scratch. Also: the 'latest package version' policy applies at each dependency's introduction/bump time, not as a promise that dependencies auto-upgrade every session.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Keeping CrazyGames as a documented-but-deferred requirement (REQ-001b/REQ-170b) was considered and rejected by explicit user choice in favor of clean removal. Leaving the existing devDependencies (typescript ^5.4.0, @types/node ^26.1.1) untouched and applying the version policy only to future packages (e.g. the Poki SDK) was also considered and rejected by explicit user choice; the existing toolchain was bumped now instead (typescript -> ^7.0.2, the latest published stable; @types/node already at latest ^26.1.1), requiring a tsconfig.json fix (moduleResolution 'Node'/node10 was removed in TS7; moved to module=CommonJS + moduleResolution=Bundler, and added an explicit compilerOptions.types:['node'] since @types/node ambient globals were no longer auto-included) — re-verified 291/291 tests green and the SDLC gate clean before landing.

decisions.title: Novelty descriptor composition fixed (resolves P5 open question 4): mechanic histogram + 4x4 geometry signature + bucketed trajectory shape; divergence = profile-weighted distance to the NEAREST corpus member; emergent-fun anchors = plain-movement-envelope escapes
decisions.why_it_exists: REQ-053 says only 'algorithmic metric targeting layout divergence' and REQ-054 only 'probe physics edge cases; flag kinetic anchors' — S5.5 had to pin the actual math or the metrics would be arbitrary.
decisions.problem_it_solves: Novelty (src/eval/gdos/Novelty.ts, PURE per dm-0037): a fixed-length descriptor — entity-kind fractions in ENTITY_KINDS order (what the level uses), solid-fraction over a 4x4 region grid (how it is shaped, resolution-independent), and the fastest completion's tape bucketed into 8 mean-moveAxis spans + normalized first-jump position + hasJump/hasTrajectory flags (how it PLAYS — two identical layouts with different jump timing are different levels). Distance = weighted mean of per-component normalized Euclidean distances, weights in ScoringProfile.novelty (dm-0031; profile v1 extended in-place — no persisted profile existed anywhere yet, so no version bump was needed; future field additions bump the version). Divergence vs a corpus = distance to the NEAREST member (novelty is distance from the closest existing thing), null on an empty corpus (content gate: no global registry exists). EmergentFun (src/eval/EmergentFun.ts, top-level per dm-0037): walks the bounded S4.2 graph and flags states escaping the plain-movement envelope — |vx| > runSpeed*factor or upward > jumpSpeed*factor, factors over TUNING so retuning re-scopes automatically; downward is never flagged (falling is gravity, not emergence). Anchors dedupe by quantized position+axis, attribute to the nearest KINETIC_KINDS entity (grouping now exported from DesignSpace, shared with the streamability gate), and carry reconstructed input frames so P7 can replay the exact moment. Housekeeping folded in: the foreign dm-0038 entry's status field was normalized from lowercase 'accepted' to the ledger-wide 'ACCEPTED' convention (content untouched) so the S5.6 strict parser can enforce a closed status set.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Renewed Uncertainty / Surprise (Section 2 arc) — novelty guards against repetition; emergent fun harvests the moments that make clips.
decisions.misconception_created: Nearest-neighbor novelty is corpus-relative: the same candidate scores differently as the campaign grows (intended — novelty IS relative to what exists). The trajectory component reads the FASTEST completion only; a level whose novelty lives in a slow alternate route needs the route-multiplicity signal (ReplayValue), not this metric.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Average-distance-to-corpus was rejected: it rewards being far from the centroid, letting a candidate that clones one existing level still score high because it is far from the others; min-distance catches exact repetition, which is REQ-051's regression concern. K-nearest averaging was rejected for now: with unit-fixture corpora k is degenerate; revisit at P10 scale via a profile field if needed. Embedding player POSITIONS in the trajectory shape was rejected: positions require replaying the tape (a sim run), which would force Novelty out of gdos/ purity for marginal signal over moveAxis buckets.

decisions.title: Executable Design Memory semantics (S5.6): v1.0 keeps rejected_ideas and lifecycle mechanics EMPTY; repetition guard = exact-title match; appends are pure with parameterized dates
decisions.why_it_exists: REQ-050/051/111 demand an executable store over meta/design_memory_ledger.json (its own header note + dm-0032), but the file format left three semantics open: what rejected_ideas holds, what a lifecycle mechanics entry looks like, and what prevents re-proposing a ledgered idea.
decisions.problem_it_solves: src/eval/gdos/DesignMemory.ts is the pure text->records->text layer: strict parse (path-qualified Result errors, closed ACCEPTED|REJECTED status set, dm-#### ids unique, YYYY-MM-DD dates, five non-empty Intent Repository fields per entry), findPriorArt (case-insensitive term search over title+fields — the REQ-051 parse-before-proposing query), nextDecisionId, pure appendDecision (returns a new document; date is a parameter, never a clock read), and canonical serialize (fixed key order, 2-space indent, trailing newline; byte-idempotent — proven against this very file). v1.0 semantics pinned: rejected ideas are decisions with status REJECTED, so rejected_ideas[] stays empty; mechanic lifecycle entries belong to P7 behind a ledger version bump, so mechanics[] stays empty; the repetition guard rejects an exact-duplicate title and directs the proposer to findPriorArt. THIS ENTRY was appended via the store itself — the executable memory is live on its own backing file.
decisions.emotion_targeted: N/A (design-infrastructure decision); indirectly protects every future creative decision from regression/repetition (Section 6).
decisions.misconception_created: The exact-title guard catches literal repetition only — a reworded duplicate passes it; findPriorArt (term search) is the real defense and stays a mandatory step in the proposal workflow, not an automatic gate. Case-insensitive SEMANTIC dedup was deliberately not attempted.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Fuzzy/semantic similarity for the repetition guard was rejected: any usefully-fuzzy matcher needs scoring thresholds that would themselves be unfalsifiable calibration, and false-positive blocks on legitimate new decisions are worse than a human-in-the-loop findPriorArt check. Allowing free-form extra fields per entry was rejected: strict keys are what let the store detect drift/corruption in a file both humans and tools edit. Storing camelCase on disk was rejected: the existing 39 entries are snake_case; the store adapts to its data, not the reverse.

decisions.title: Curation semantics (S5.7): REQ-012 kill proxy = a completion tape that never presses THE jump; the six REQ-022 pruning questions defined as data; qualitative First-Party criteria are attestations with mandatory rationales
decisions.why_it_exists: REQ-020/021/022 name the machinery (Kill Switch, First-Party gate, subtraction pass) but not its decision rules, and REQ-012's P5 share ('every challenge tests the one-jump constraint') needed a computable evidence proxy.
decisions.problem_it_solves: src/eval/gdos/Curation.ts (PURE, dm-0037). Kill Switch grounds: failed GDOS gates; a completing run whose tape contains no jumpPressed frame (if the level is completable without THE jump it does not isolate/test the constraint — REQ-012 becomes evidence-checkable from the tapes already in the bundle); adding a mechanic while the REQ-042 economy comparison favors deepening. Every kill records the full five Intent Repository fields so the rejection enters the Design Memory and cannot be re-proposed unimproved. First-Party review: the three Section-3 criteria are typed Attestations {affirmed, rationale} with the rationale MANDATORY — an unexplained judgement fails the review by construction; the verdict logic over attestations (+ optional GdosReport) is deterministic. Subtractive engine: the six pruning questions are DATA (elevates-core, degrades-if-removed, non-duplicative, earns-complexity, self-explanatory, build-again); any NO makes a removal candidate, any missing answer leaves the pass incomplete — silence is not a keep.
decisions.emotion_targeted: The whole arc, by exclusion: curation is how non-elevating content never reaches a player.
decisions.misconception_created: The jump-free-completion proxy is necessary, not sufficient: a level where the jump is pressed but irrelevant (pressable anywhere, changes nothing) passes the proxy; catching that requires a counterfactual no-jump solvability search, deliberately deferred to the P7 pipeline where the search budget already exists. The six question TEXTS are v1 wording; editing them is a ledgered decision, not a tweak, because past pass results implicitly reference them.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Deriving REQ-012 from solvability-with-forbidden-jump (search with a no-jump constraint) was rejected for S5.7: it re-runs a search inside gdos/ (violates dm-0037) or forces another top-level module for marginal gain now — the tape proxy uses evidence already assembled. Scoring the First-Party criteria numerically was rejected: pretending Self-Explanation is a computation is exactly the pseudo-measurement dm-0031 exists to prevent. Making unanswered subtractive questions default to KEEP was rejected: a subtraction pass whose silence means keep decays into a rubber stamp.

decisions.title: CDRE mining rules (S5.8): symmetric threshold mining (lower AND raise), zero-score means a dead estimator not a strict bar, cross-profile scores are skipped, and apply() versions profileId only
decisions.why_it_exists: dm-0033 pinned CDRE to a profile-evolution loop but left the mining RULES open. Written naively, CDRE becomes a Goodhart ratchet: it observes failing gates, lowers the bars, and the scoring engine converges on passing everything.
decisions.problem_it_solves: src/eval/gdos/Cdre.ts mines four proposal kinds in a fixed deterministic order. THRESHOLD-ADJUSTMENT is SYMMETRIC: a metric failing at least failureRateForLowering of reports proposes lowering to the configured percentile of observed scores, AND a metric passing every report by at least raiseMarginPoints proposes RAISING to the weakest observed score. The raise direction is the load-bearing half — mining only failures would ratchet the gates toward vacuity. ESTIMATOR-UNMEASURABLE: a metric scoring exactly 0 in every report is an evidence-source defect (dm-0036) that no threshold change can fix, so none is proposed. COVERAGE-GAP: axis values never exercised (REQ-041). RECURRING-REJECTION: one kill reason dominating the decision record is a systemic design problem upstream, not a bar to relax. Guards: scores gathered under differing thresholds are SKIPPED (cross-profile scores are incomparable per dm-0031); the info-density metrics (peakScreenDensity band test, failureVisibility zero-limit test) are never threshold-mined because percentile recalibration is meaningless for a metric that is not a >= cutoff; samples below minReports mine nothing. apply() accepts ONLY an ACCEPTED threshold-adjustment (a PROPOSED or REJECTED proposal is inert), is pure, versions the profileId and never PROFILE_SCHEMA_VERSION (the calibration changed, the format did not — honoring the spent in-place-extension grace from dm-0039), and round-trips the evolved record through parseProfile so an invalid evolution fails loudly instead of poisoning the engine.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Protects every Section-6 emotional and streamability target by keeping their gates meaningful bars rather than formalities that drift toward whatever the content already achieves.
decisions.misconception_created: CDRE cannot distinguish a miscalibrated bar from bad content — a lowering proposal is a QUESTION for the acceptor, not a recommendation. Raising to the weakest observed score calibrates to the present corpus, so a future legitimate outlier would now fail. Proposals are observations; only an explicit human/GDOS acceptance changes anything.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Auto-applying accepted-looking proposals was rejected (dm-0033: an apply is an explicit, ledgered act). Mining only the failing direction was rejected as the Goodhart ratchet described above. Learning thresholds statistically from outcome data was rejected: no player data exists before P8 telemetry, and any fitted model would break determinism and the zero-dependency invariant. Bumping PROFILE_SCHEMA_VERSION on every apply was rejected: an evolved calibration is not a new FORMAT, and versioning the schema per threshold tweak would make the dm-0010 migration policy meaningless.

decisions.title: P6 Campaign Director is a pure fold over frame-derived behavioral signals, never a mutable rolling object (dm-0043)
decisions.why_it_exists: The naive Campaign Director keeps a mutable CampaignState object and patches fields in place per level, which violates dm-0004 (never mutate state in place) generalized to all state records, and behavioral signal derivation is at risk of using wall-clock timestamps, which would violate dm-0003 (fixed step, no delta-time scaling) and break cross-machine determinism.
decisions.problem_it_solves: The P6 campaign-state update is a pure fold step updateState(state, record, profile) -> CampaignState, always returning a new record; ZERO_CAMPAIGN_STATE is the additive identity and processCampaign(records, profile) is records.reduce(updateState, ZERO_CAMPAIGN_STATE) with no mutation and no side effects. Every behavioral signal in BehaviorSignals is expressed in frame counts derived from ReplayTape.frames only: hesitation is the longest gap of no-input frames before a jump input, panic burst is N inputs within M consecutive frames, commitment speed is the frame index of the first jump input, drop-off is the tape ending without the goal-reached event. These are reproducible purely from the tape with no clock read.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Protects the determinism guarantee the whole engine depends on (dm-0003/dm-0004/dm-0019): a campaign report computed twice from the same records must be bit-identical, which is what lets S6.5 assert processCampaign is deterministic.
decisions.misconception_created: A rolling fold looks superficially like a stateful object with the same field names; readers must not assume updateState mutates its input — every call returns a fresh CampaignState and the previous one remains valid and unchanged (referentially transparent).
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: A mutable event-stream/observer model was considered (would allow decoupled subscriber updates) but rejected: subscriber ordering is a non-determinism source and it violates the stateless-system invariant the whole codebase holds. Deriving behavioral signals from wall-clock timestamps captured during a live run was rejected outright: it would make identical replays produce different campaign state depending on machine speed, which the entire replay-tape architecture (dm-0023) exists to prevent.

decisions.title: REQ-032 P6/P8 contract boundary: BehaviorSignals is a typed data record, TapeAnalyzer derives it without P8 telemetry (dm-0044)
decisions.why_it_exists: REQ-032 (Player Behavior Model) is co-owned by P6 and P8, but P8 (live browser telemetry capture) does not exist yet and P6 cannot be blocked on it. A naive CampaignDirector coupled directly to a concrete telemetry collector would either ship P6 half-built pending P8, or force a P6 refactor the moment P8 lands.
decisions.problem_it_solves: P6 defines BehaviorSignals as a typed data record and TapeAnalyzer.analyzeTape(tape: ReplayTape, profile: CampaignProfile) -> BehaviorSignals as the P8-independent derivation path from replay tapes. CampaignDirector and every campaign/ module depend only on the BehaviorSignals record shape, never on its source. When P8 lands, it supplies live-collected signals as an alternative implementation of the identical record shape -- a data substitution, not a structural change to campaign/.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Removes the anxiety of a phase-ordering deadlock: P6 can close and M2 can verify without P8 existing, exactly as the IRD phase DAG requires (P6 before P8 in delivery order but REQ-032 spans both).
decisions.misconception_created: BehaviorSignals looks like "the real telemetry format" but it is a P6-owned data contract; P8 must conform to this shape (or a ledgered version of it), not the other way around -- P6 does not get retrofitted to match whatever P8 eventually invents.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Defining an abstract TelemetrySource interface with a mock implementation for P6 and a real implementation deferred to P8 was considered, but rejected as unnecessary indirection (YAGNI): TapeAnalyzer already IS the P8-independent implementation because replay tapes are literally full input recordings; there is no need for a seam beyond the BehaviorSignals record type itself. Blocking P6 until P8 ships was rejected: it would stall M2 indefinitely since P8 is a P3-milestone-later phase.

decisions.title: MechanicTracker consumes CoverageMatrix by reference; CampaignProfile is a separate versioned schema from ScoringProfile (dm-0045)
decisions.why_it_exists: P5 already owns coverageMatrix(evidence[]) -> CoverageMatrix as the single source of truth for the design-space axes (dm-0034); a P6 MechanicTracker that re-derives or duplicates that matrix would create two sources of truth that can drift. Separately, extending ScoringProfile with campaign-level calibration (spike thresholds, mastery weights, retention multipliers) would couple two distinct concerns and force a PROFILE_SCHEMA_VERSION bump under the now-spent in-place-extension grace (dm-0039).
decisions.problem_it_solves: MechanicTracker.trackMechanics(state, record, coverageMatrix, profile) receives CoverageMatrix as a parameter passed in by the caller; it never imports or calls DesignSpace.coverageMatrix() itself. "Mechanics introduced" means at least one level record covers a coverage-matrix cell for that mechanic; "mechanics mastered" means the mechanic appears in the top optimization tier AND the exploration-archetype routing confidence meets profile.mastery.masteryRoutingConfidenceThreshold. CampaignProfile is its own versioned data structure (campaignProfileSchemaVersion 1), strict-parsed with the full P2 discipline, with its own frozen DEFAULT_CAMPAIGN_PROFILE -- completely independent of ScoringProfile and PROFILE_SCHEMA_VERSION.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Protects REQ-041 (coverage matrix single source of truth) from silent drift between the P5 computation and a P6 shadow computation that could diverge under a code change to one but not the other.
decisions.misconception_created: Because CampaignProfile mirrors ScoringProfile's strict-parse discipline field-for-field in spirit, a reader might assume the two schemas are versioned together; they are not -- bumping PROFILE_SCHEMA_VERSION never implies bumping campaignProfileSchemaVersion and vice versa.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Extending ScoringProfile in place with campaign fields was rejected because the extension grace is spent (dm-0039) and because campaign calibration is a genuinely separate concern from per-level GDOS scoring calibration -- coupling them would mean a threshold-adjustment CDRE proposal for a GDOS gate and a campaign spike-sensitivity retune would compete for the same versioned document. Re-deriving coverage inside campaign/ from raw GdosReport data was rejected as duplicated logic that could diverge from DesignSpace.coverageMatrix.

decisions.title: REQ-142 elevated from per-session verdict to running campaign trajectory; retentionPrediction scoped as an observable-behavior proxy (dm-0046)
decisions.why_it_exists: P4 computed the four REQ-142 macro criteria (Cognitive Structural Mapping, Cross-Chapter Degradation Analysis, Curiosity Progression Curves, Graduation Assessment Verification) as a one-shot MacroVerdict for a single fixture curriculum run, explicitly leaving the P6 remainder open ("P6 contributes the campaign models"). Separately, naming a field "retention prediction" risks scope inflation toward ML or player-psychology modeling, which the project has no data, dependencies, or determinism budget for.
decisions.problem_it_solves: ChapterHealth.aggregateChapterHealth ingests a chapter's MacroVerdict records (P4 output, passed as data, never re-computed) and lifts each of the four criteria to a trend line stored in CampaignState.chapterHealth -- P6 computes the delta between adjacent chapters' verdicts and stores the trajectory, rather than re-running the P4 criteria. retentionPrediction is scoped explicitly as a CampaignProfile-weighted composite of observable behavioral signals (retry cadence, optimization depth trend, panic-cycle frequency, curiosity trend, chapter health), documented as a proxy for whether the design is rewarding the player -- not a claim about player intent, psychology, or future action. Its weights live in CampaignProfile.retentionWeights.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Keeps REQ-142 honest as a design-elevation exercise (trend over verdicts) rather than a re-audit, and keeps retentionPrediction defensible as a documented design proxy rather than an overclaimed prediction the project cannot actually validate without live players.
decisions.misconception_created: retentionPrediction sounds like it forecasts whether an actual player will return; it does not -- it is a weighted read of frame-derived behavioral signals against the current calibration, exactly as honest as a GDOS "curiosity" score is a proxy for delivered curiosity, not measured player emotion (dm-0031 precedent).
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Re-running the four REQ-142 criteria from scratch at the campaign level (ignoring the P4 MacroVerdict) was rejected as duplicated audit logic and a violation of the P4-P6 handoff (P6 consumes P4 verdicts as data). A statistically fitted retention model was rejected: no live player data exists before P8, and any fitted model breaks the zero-dependency and determinism invariants -- the CDRE precedent (dm-0033) established that recalibration is a data change under version control, not a re-trained model.

decisions.title: CampaignDirector lives at src/eval/campaign/, a new top-level sibling of gdos/ -- consumes GdosReport as data, never touches gdos/ internals (dm-0047)
decisions.why_it_exists: The gdos/ sub-tree is scan-enforced PURE over pre-assembled evidence: it runs no sim, no search, no audit, and the EvalIsolation scan checks gdos/ imports no AgentHarness/Evaluate/local-Search/core-Engine (dm-0037). The CampaignDirector operates at a strictly higher level of abstraction than per-level scoring -- it folds over a sequence of already-judged levels -- and putting it inside gdos/ would either violate that purity boundary or force an exception into the existing scan.
decisions.problem_it_solves: All P6 code lives in src/eval/campaign/, a new top-level sub-folder of src/eval/ alongside Evaluate.ts and EmergentFun.ts, not inside gdos/. campaign/ imports only P5 output data types (GdosReport, CoverageMatrix, ReplayTape, MacroVerdict) via `import type`, never gdos/Emotional.ts, gdos/InfoDensity.ts, or any gate-internal module, and never AgentHarness/Evaluate/Search/Engine (no re-auditing, mirroring the gdos/ rule for the same reason: campaign/ consumes verdicts as data, it never re-runs the audits or the scoring gates). The existing EvalIsolation gdos/ scans are unaffected because campaign/ is a sibling, not a gdos/ member.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Preserves confidence that the "gdos/ is pure" invariant (dm-0037, proven by two scans) stays total with zero carve-outs, the same value the original EmergentFun siting decision protected.
decisions.misconception_created: campaign/ sitting next to gdos/ under src/eval/ might suggest it is part of the scoring engine; it is not -- it is a consumer of the scoring engine's output, one level of abstraction above judgeLevel, exactly as Evaluate.ts sits one level below it (assembling evidence) rather than inside it.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Placing CampaignDirector inside gdos/ was rejected: it would either force the EvalIsolation purity scans to special-case a "campaign" exception (undermining the totality the scans currently guarantee) or misrepresent campaign/ as pure-over-one-bundle when it is actually a fold over many bundles' worth of reports. A single monolithic src/eval/CampaignAnalyzer.ts module merging state, behavior extraction, mechanic tracking, chapter health and direction was rejected as violating per-concern testability -- behavioral extraction could not be unit-tested independently of chapter health, mirroring the same system-isolation argument that split P5 into Evidence/Profile/Report/DesignSpace/gates rather than one file.

decisions.title: Difficulty-spike detection uses an EMA baseline and profiled drop threshold, never a hardcoded heuristic; campaign design-space alternatives rejected (dm-0048)
decisions.why_it_exists: A difficulty spike is informally "chapter health drops sharply from the rolling baseline," but "sharply" must not be a hardcoded constant -- that would be exactly the magic-number creep dm-0031 eliminated from GDOS gates, now reappearing one layer up in the campaign director.
decisions.problem_it_solves: CampaignDirector computes a rolling chapterHealthBaseline as an exponential moving average with a decay factor from CampaignProfile.chapterHealthCalibration.baselineDecay, and flags a spike -- emitting a typed CampaignAlert record (kind, chapterId, reason, contributing findings; same evidentiary pattern as DesignDecision in P5, never a bare boolean) -- when the current chapter's health falls below baseline minus profile.chapterHealthCalibration.spikeDropThreshold. Both the decay factor and the drop threshold are CampaignProfile fields, so recalibrating spike sensitivity is a data change, never a code edit. Alternatives considered and rejected for the wider campaign design: a JSON-rules interpreter for spike/retention logic (accidental complexity -- the thresholds are already externalized as CampaignProfile data, so an interpreter buys nothing YAGNI would keep); and P6 code importing P4 audit functions directly from src/eval/local/ to recompute chapter signals (would re-run the sim -- P6 consumes P4's LevelRecord/MacroVerdict output as data, exactly as the P4-to-P5 and P5-to-P6 handoffs already established).
decisions.emotion_targeted: Protects the "zero numeric literals in gate logic" validation criterion (dm-0031, extended to P6 by dm-0045) from being quietly violated one layer above the GDOS gates, where it would be easy to rationalize a hardcoded threshold as "just campaign glue code."
decisions.misconception_created: A spike alert firing does not mean the chapter is broken or unsolvable -- P4's local audits already guarantee every level in the chapter is solvable, softlock-free and exploit-free; a CampaignAlert is a design-quality signal (this chapter reads harder than the established baseline), not a correctness failure.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: A fixed-window (non-EMA) baseline of the last N chapters was considered but rejected in favor of an EMA: a fixed window requires an arbitrary N and produces a discontinuous baseline jump when the window slides past an old outlier chapter, whereas an EMA smooths continuously and only needs one profiled decay factor. Statistical anomaly detection (z-score against the full campaign's health distribution) was rejected as premature: the profiled EMA-threshold approach is simpler, deterministic, and sufficient for the REQ-030 exit condition (flag a synthetic difficulty spike on a fixture campaign); it can be superseded later via a ledgered CampaignProfile evolution if real campaign data shows it insufficient.

decisions.title: S6.2 corrections: LevelRecord carries ArchetypeRun (not bare ReplayTape) for outcome/attempts; platformCheckCount redefined as an input-pattern proxy, not level geometry (dm-0049)
decisions.why_it_exists: S6.1 shipped LevelRecord.tape typed as a bare ReplayTape and CampaignProfile.behavior.platformCheckLedgeDistanceTiles assuming ledge-distance geometry would be available to TapeAnalyzer. Both were flagged as open questions in the S6.1 handoff. Zero-assumption inspection at S6.2 start found two concrete problems: (1) ReplayTape has no outcome/attempts fields, so dropOffRate (goal reached or not) and retryCount (reload count) cannot be derived from it -- that data exists only on ArchetypeRun (gdos/Evidence.ts), which the P4 agent harness already computes per run. (2) ReplayTape carries no level geometry at all (it is pure input: schemaVersion/levelId/seed/frames), so a ledge-distance-based platformCheckCount is unbuildable without also threading LevelDefinition through TapeAnalyzer, adding a geometry dependency campaign/ does not otherwise need.
decisions.problem_it_solves: LevelRecord.tape: ReplayTape is replaced with LevelRecord.run: ArchetypeRun (gdos/Evidence.ts, import type only) -- ArchetypeRun already bundles outcome/attempts/ticksElapsed/tape in one P4-harness-produced record, so TapeAnalyzer.analyzeTape(run: ArchetypeRun, profile: CampaignProfile) reads retryCount directly from run.attempts (the harness-authoritative reload count) and dropOffRate from run.outcome === "timeout" ? 1 : 0, with zero re-derivation. platformCheckCount is redefined as a pure input-pattern proxy: a moveAxis direction reversal (a new nonzero direction differing from the last nonzero direction) that has no jumpPressed edge within the following profile.behavior.platformCheckWindowFrames frames counts as one platform check -- "the player approached then backed off instead of committing to a jump," derivable from frames alone. CampaignProfile.behavior.platformCheckLedgeDistanceTiles is renamed to platformCheckWindowFrames (a frame count, not a tile distance), keeping every BehaviorSignals field frame-derived per dm-0043 with zero geometry coupling.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Protects the P8-independence boundary (dm-0044) an extra degree: TapeAnalyzer now depends only on ArchetypeRun (harness output, already P8-independent) and CampaignProfile, never on LevelDefinition -- so a future geometry-aware refinement of platform-checking is an additive option, not a retrofit of a boundary that was wrong from S6.1.
decisions.misconception_created: platformCheckCount sounds like it detects the player standing near a physical ledge; it does not -- it detects a direction-reversal INPUT PATTERN typical of ledge-checking behavior, which is a proxy exactly as honest as GDOS's own emotional estimators (dm-0031): it measures the input signature, not the geometric fact. A future P8/P9 refinement could cross-reference actual player position against tile geometry for a tighter signal; this baseline is deliberately geometry-free.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Threading LevelDefinition into LevelRecord and TapeAnalyzer so platformCheckCount could measure real ledge distance was considered and rejected for S6.2: it adds a second input type (geometry) to a module whose whole design point (dm-0044) is depending on nothing but frame-indexed behavioral data, for a signal whose input-pattern proxy is already a reasonable, testable stand-in. Re-deriving retryCount by counting resetPressed edges in the tape (rather than reading run.attempts) was considered and rejected: run.attempts is the harness's own authoritative count of the exact same event, already computed once at S4.2-S4.5 -- recomputing it from raw frames would risk drift between two representations of one fact and violates the "consume P4 output as data, do not re-derive" precedent that governs every P5/P6 handoff.

decisions.title: S6.3 corrections: LevelRecord gains mechanicsExercised (caller-supplied); mastery routing-confidence is a coverage-cell top-tier ratio; CampaignState mechanic sets widened to EntityKind (dm-0050)
decisions.why_it_exists: MechanicTracker (REQ-031 mechanics-introduced/mastered, REQ-041 P6 coverage share) needs to know which mechanics a SPECIFIC level exercises to update per-mechanic knowledge incrementally, but none of LevelRecord's existing fields (GdosReport, ArchetypeRun, MacroVerdict) carry a mechanic list -- that lives on EvidenceBundle/LevelDefinition, which campaign/ must not import (dm-0047: no re-auditing, no geometry coupling). Separately, S6.1's masteryRoutingConfidenceThreshold was defined as a vague "exploration-archetype routing confidence" with no concrete derivation -- zero-assumption inspection at S6.3 found LevelRecord carries only ONE ArchetypeRun (not one per archetype), so archetype-identity-based routing confidence is unbuildable from the available data without widening LevelRecord further, which the coverage matrix already makes unnecessary.
decisions.problem_it_solves: LevelRecord gains `mechanicsExercised: ReadonlySet<EntityKind>` -- the caller (whoever assembles LevelRecord from EvidenceBundle, upstream of campaign/) supplies this via DesignSpace.ts's existing extractCoverage(bundle), mapped to just the mechanic segment of each cell key; campaign/ never derives it itself, staying free of EvidenceBundle/LevelDefinition imports. masteryRoutingConfidenceThreshold is precisely redefined (not renamed) as: of a mechanic's distinct exercised design-space cells in the running CoverageMatrix, the fraction that sit at the top optimization tier ('worldRecord', the last OPTIMIZATION_STYLE_AXIS entry) must meet or exceed this threshold for mastery -- a real ratio computed purely from CoverageMatrix.cells' canonical "mechanic|environment|emotion|optimizationStyle|playerType" keys, no archetype-identity ambiguity. CampaignProfile.mastery gains knowledgeLearningRate (0,1] for KnowledgeModel's per-mechanic confidence EMA (target 1 on a passing+completed level, 0 otherwise) -- the same EMA technique as chapterHealthCalibration.baselineDecay (dm-0048), applied one layer down. CampaignState.mechanicsIntroduced/mechanicsMastered are widened from ReadonlySet<string> to ReadonlySet<EntityKind> (import type only from components/Behavior, mirroring gdos/DesignSpace.ts's own precedent) now that MechanicTracker genuinely produces EntityKind-typed sets from CoverageMatrix.mechanicsCovered -- avoiding a lossy string cast at the campaign/gdos boundary.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Keeps REQ-041's "coverage matrix by reference, one source of truth" guarantee (dm-0045) intact even as MechanicTracker needs finer per-level granularity than the aggregate CoverageMatrix alone provides -- the fix is a caller-supplied fact (mechanicsExercised), not a second coverage computation inside campaign/.
decisions.misconception_created: mechanicsExercised on LevelRecord might look like campaign/ is now geometry-aware; it is not -- the field is an opaque caller-supplied EntityKind set, exactly as GdosReport/ArchetypeRun/MacroVerdict already are caller-assembled P4/P5 evidence. campaign/ still never imports LevelDefinition, EvidenceBundle, or any geometry type itself.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Widening LevelRecord to carry the FULL EvidenceBundle (which already has mechanicsOf-equivalent extraction internally via DesignSpace.extractCoverage) was rejected: it would pull deaths/verdicts/tapes-for-all-archetypes into campaign/'s input surface for a single mechanic-list fact, far more coupling than needed, and duplicates data already summarized in the coverage matrix. Deriving "routing confidence" from LevelRecord.run.archetype identity (checking if run.archetype === 'curiousExplorer') was rejected: LevelRecord carries exactly one ArchetypeRun per record with no guarantee of which archetype it represents, so an identity check would silently misclassify records built from a different archetype's run -- the coverage-cell ratio needs no archetype identity at all and is robust to that ambiguity.

decisions.title: S6.4 ChapterHealth: score is a structural pass-count over MacroVerdict (not a calibrated weight), trend needs a new profiled tolerance, alerts stay empty here (dm-0051)
decisions.why_it_exists: REQ-142's P6 share (dm-0046) elevates a chapter's four macro criteria plus chapterHealthy from a one-shot MacroVerdict (P4 output) to a running campaign trajectory. Turning five booleans into one score needs SOME aggregation rule, and turning two adjacent scores into a rising/falling/flat trend needs a tolerance -- both must avoid inventing hardcoded magic numbers (dm-0031's zero-calibration-literals discipline, carried into P6 by dm-0045). Separately, ChapterHealth was drafted with a chapterRecords parameter in the S6.3 handoff's S6.4 preview that on implementation proved unused: the four criteria and chapterHealthy all come from macroVerdict directly (per dm-0046, never re-derived), so nothing in aggregateChapterHealth needs the raw per-level records.
decisions.problem_it_solves: chapterScore(macroVerdict) = (count of {chapterHealthy, cognitiveStructuralMapping.pass, crossChapterDegradation.pass, curiosityProgression.pass, graduationAssessment.pass} that are true) / 5 * 100 -- a STRUCTURAL aggregation (every MacroVerdict has exactly these five boolean components, by its own type definition) rather than a calibrated weighting, so it needs no profile field: the "5 equal components" is a fact of MacroVerdict's shape, not a tunable design choice, the same way GdosReport.pass = ALL gates passing is structural, not weighted. computeTrend(current, prior, tolerance) DOES need calibration: CampaignProfile.chapterHealthCalibration gains `trendFlatTolerance` (a score-point band within which two adjacent chapters count as "flat" rather than rising/falling) -- an actually-tunable design choice, correctly externalized. aggregateChapterHealth(macroVerdict, priorChapterHealth, profile) drops the unused chapterRecords parameter entirely (no debt: an unread parameter is exactly as much a smell as an unread profile field, dm-0049's hesitationFrameThreshold precedent). `alerts` is always `[]` on ChapterHealth's own output: spike detection needs the rolling EMA baseline across ALL chapters processed so far, which only CampaignDirector (S6.5) accumulates during its fold -- ChapterHealth reports one chapter's own score and trend, nothing more, and CampaignDirector is the sole author of CampaignAlert records.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Protects the zero-calibration-literals discipline from a subtle failure mode: treating a STRUCTURAL fact (MacroVerdict always has exactly 5 boolean components) as if it needed a tunable weight would either invent a meaningless profile field or hardcode a literal disguised as "obviously always 1/5 each" -- this decision draws the line explicitly so future slices know which is which.
decisions.misconception_created: A reader might expect ChapterHealth to compute alerts since it is the module that produces per-chapter health; it deliberately does not -- alerts require cross-chapter state (the rolling baseline) that only exists inside CampaignDirector's fold, so ChapterHealth::alerts staying empty is correct behavior, not an unfinished feature.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Weighting chapterHealthy and the four criteria unevenly (e.g. chapterHealthy worth more since it is a precondition) was considered and rejected: no PRD or REQ text motivates unequal weights, and inventing one would be exactly the kind of ungrounded pseudo-measurement dm-0031 exists to prevent -- equal weighting is the honest default until evidence motivates otherwise, revisable via a ledgered profile change. Passing chapterRecords into aggregateChapterHealth "for future use" was rejected as speculative generality (the project's own no-debt principle: build what is needed now, not what might be needed later). Computing alerts inside ChapterHealth by comparing only the current and prior chapter (a 2-chapter window instead of the true EMA baseline) was rejected: it would silently produce a DIFFERENT spike-detection algorithm than CampaignDirector's EMA-baseline design (dm-0048), duplicating and diverging from the one spike-detection rule the campaign should have.

decisions.title: S6.5 CampaignDirector: chapter-grouped fold input, optimizationDepth from cumulative coverage, emotionalState rolled from GDOS's own gate scores, trendWindowLevels converted to an EMA rate (not left dead), retentionPrediction's five-component normalization (dm-0052)
decisions.why_it_exists: REQ-030 (the Game Director folding level-by-level state) and the four still-unbuilt REQ-031 macro variables (emotionalState, skillCurve, optimizationDepth, curiosityTrend, retentionPrediction) all land in one slice, and each needed a concrete data source that was not yet verified. ChapterHealth (S6.4) is chapter-scoped while TapeAnalyzer/MechanicTracker/KnowledgeModel (S6.2/S6.3) are level-scoped, so a flat LevelRecord[] cannot drive processCampaign alone. optimizationDepth has no per-level numeric source on LevelRecord (GdosReport carries only gate pass/fail + named MetricScores, never a raw optimization-tier index; that lives in the P4 local Optimization audit, which campaign/ must not re-run per dm-0047). emotionalState risked becoming a second, less-honest proxy for something GDOS's emotional-threshold gate already estimates properly per level (dm-0031's Surprise/Confidence/Mastery/Curiosity semantics). trendWindowLevels (shipped at S6.1, unused through S6.4) was at real risk of becoming a THIRD dead-calibration field (after S6.2's hesitationFrameThreshold and none since, both caught before being called done) unless S6.5 gave it a genuine consumer.
decisions.problem_it_solves: CampaignDirector.processCampaign(chapters: readonly ChapterRecord[], profile) -> CampaignReport, where ChapterRecord = { chapterId, levels: readonly LevelRecord[], macroVerdict: MacroVerdict, coverageMatrix: CoverageMatrix (cumulative through this chapter) } -- the caller groups levels by chapter and supplies the chapter's MacroVerdict once, matching P4's own macro-pass granularity. updateState(state, record, coverageMatrix, profile) is the per-LEVEL fold step (behaviorState/knowledgeState/mechanicsIntroduced/mechanicsMastered/optimizationDepth); ChapterHealth.aggregateChapterHealth runs once per chapter after its levels fold. optimizationDepth = (index of the highest tier in the CUMULATIVE coverageMatrix.optimizationStylesCovered + 1) / OPTIMIZATION_STYLE_AXIS.length -- reusing the SAME coverageMatrix parameter MechanicTracker already receives (dm-0045: one source of truth), importing OPTIMIZATION_STYLE_AXIS as a VALUE from gdos/DesignSpace.ts (a frozen zero-behavior registry array, the same "read the registry as data" allowance dm-0034 already grants gdos/ itself for ARCHETYPES/ENTITY_KINDS -- not a gate-internal import, so dm-0047's "import type only from gdos OUTPUT types" restriction does not apply to this specific registry constant). emotionalState is EMA-rolled from the REAL per-level scores GDOS's own 'emotional-threshold' gate already computed (record.report.gates.find(g => g.gate === 'emotional-threshold').scores, metrics named curiosity/confidence/surprise/mastery, each /100 to normalize to [0,1]) -- never re-derived from behavioral signals, which would have been a second, less-grounded proxy for something already honestly estimated. trendWindowLevels is converted to an EMA decay rate via the standard N-period formula rate = 2/(trendWindowLevels+1), now GENUINELY consumed by emotionalState/skillCurve/curiosityTrend's rolling updates -- no dead field. A new top-level CampaignProfile field, trendMagnitudeTolerance, gates rising/falling/flat classification for skillCurve/curiosityTrend (both operate on [0,1]-normalized values, distinct in scale from chapterHealthCalibration.trendFlatTolerance which is chapter-SCORE-scaled [0,100] -- reusing that field across scales would silently miscalibrate one of them). skillCurve trends an "ease" reading (1/(1+run.attempts), EMA-rolled) via direct step-over-step comparison against the PRIOR CampaignState's ease EMA (no history buffer needed -- a fold naturally has the prior state in scope). curiosityTrend trends emotionalState.curiosity the same way. retentionPrediction is profile.retentionWeights' weighted average of five [0,1]-normalized components: retryCadence = 1 - behaviorState.dropOffRate; optimizationDepth = state.optimizationDepth directly (already [0,1]); panicCycles = 1/(1+behaviorState.panicBurstCount); curiosityTrend = 1/0.5/0 for rising/flat/falling; chapterHealth = mean(chapterHealth[*].score)/100 (0 if no chapter closed yet). Spike detection in processCampaign finally implements the EMA-baseline design dm-0048 specified: baseline updates by chapterHealthCalibration.baselineDecay each chapter, a CampaignAlert fires when baseline - currentScore exceeds spikeDropThreshold, attached to both that chapter's ChapterHealthReport.alerts and CampaignReport.alerts.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Protects two invariants simultaneously under real implementation pressure: dm-0045's "coverage matrix and P4/P5 outputs consumed by reference, never re-derived" (optimizationDepth and emotionalState both reuse existing P5 computations rather than inventing parallel ones), and the zero-calibration-literals discipline (every new tunable value -- trendMagnitudeTolerance -- is a profile field, and the near-miss of a third dead field is closed by giving trendWindowLevels a real formula-backed consumer instead of quietly dropping it).
decisions.misconception_created: optimizationDepth reads as "how skilled is the player" but it is actually "how much of the design space's optimization axis has this CAMPAIGN's coverage matrix touched so far" -- a campaign-wide breadth measure, not a per-player mastery score (that role belongs to knowledgeState/mechanicsMastered instead). emotionalState is a rolling EMA of GDOS's own per-level delivered-emotion estimates, so it inherits every epistemic caveat those estimators already carry (dm-0031: proxies, not measured player emotion) -- rolling them up does not make them more objective, only smoother.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Deriving emotionalState fresh from behavioral signals (e.g. confidence from dropOffRate, surprise from retryCount) was considered and rejected: GDOS already computed honestly-grounded per-level emotional estimates with documented epistemics (dm-0031/dm-0036), and building a second, cruder proxy from raw behavior would both duplicate effort and produce a LESS trustworthy signal than simply rolling up the existing one. Adding a small history-buffer array to CampaignState to support true N-level windowed trending (rather than step-over-step EMA comparison) was rejected: it would grow the frozen ten-macro-variable design (dm-0043) with an eleventh implementation-detail field, where a fold-native step-over-step EMA achieves the same "is this rising or falling" signal with zero extra state. Leaving trendWindowLevels unused and adding a wholly separate emaRate field was rejected: the 2/(N+1) conversion is the standard, well-understood way to express a mean-reversion window as an EMA rate, and it closes a real dead-field risk instead of creating a new duplicate-meaning field beside it. Reusing chapterHealthCalibration.trendFlatTolerance for skillCurve/curiosityTrend was rejected: that field is calibrated against chapter-health's 0-100 score scale, applying it to a [0,1]-normalized ease/curiosity delta would silently misclassify nearly everything as "flat".

decisions.title: S6.6: campaign/ isolation (dm-0047) gets structural scan enforcement in EvalIsolation.test.ts, not just prose (dm-0053)
decisions.why_it_exists: dm-0047 stated "campaign/ never re-audits" and was re-cited in dm-0049/dm-0050/dm-0051/dm-0052 across every P6 slice, but until S6.6 it was enforced only by discipline during code review, never by a test -- the same gap the project's own gdos/ and macro/ isolation rules closed with test/unit/EvalIsolation.test.ts back at P4/P5. An unenforced architectural rule is exactly the kind of thing that erodes silently as P7/P8 start building against campaign/'s output and a future change accidentally reaches into a gdos gate internal or the local audit pass.
decisions.problem_it_solves: Four new tests added to test/unit/EvalIsolation.test.ts, mirroring the existing gdos/ scans structurally: (1) the math whitelist (dm-0017) now scans src/eval/campaign/ explicitly (the parent src/eval/ scan does not recurse into subdirectories, so campaign/ was previously uncovered by ANY math-whitelist test -- a real gap found while writing this decision, not just a hypothetical one); (2) campaign/ may not import AgentHarness/AgentPolicy/Archetypes/Evaluate/eval-local/core-Engine (no sim, no search, no local audit re-run); (3) campaign/ may not import any gdos/ GATE-internal module (Emotional/Streamability/InfoDensity/Curation/Cdre/DesignMemory/Judge/Profile) -- only Report.ts/Evidence.ts/DesignSpace.ts are allowed, and Profile.ts is explicitly forbidden as a structural enforcement of dm-0045's "CampaignProfile is a separate schema from ScoringProfile, never import it"; (4) MacroVerdict from eval/macro/ must be imported as a type only, mirroring gdos/'s identical rule for local/ verdicts. All four scans ran clean against the as-built campaign/ (7 files, S6.1-S6.5) with zero changes needed to production code -- confirming the prose rule was actually honored throughout, not just claimed.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Converts five ledger entries' worth of stated intent into a provable, CI-enforced guarantee before P6 closes and P7/P8 start depending on campaign/ — the same trust the gdos/ purity scans (dm-0031/dm-0037) already gave the P5 architecture.
decisions.misconception_created: These scans do not prove campaign/ is CORRECT, only that it is ISOLATED the way dm-0047 requires -- a campaign/ module could still compute a wrong formula while passing every one of these import-discipline checks. Isolation and correctness are separate guarantees; the CampaignDirector/ChapterHealth/etc. behavior tests (S6.1-S6.5) are what prove correctness.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Leaving dm-0047 as convention-only (as it had been through S6.1-S6.5) was rejected at phase close: the project's own precedent (gdos/ and macro/ both got scans at their introduction, not deferred) argues against treating campaign/ differently, and P6 closing is the natural checkpoint before other phases start depending on the module's stability. Writing the scan as one big combined test covering all four checks was rejected in favor of four focused tests (mirroring gdos/'s own split into "math whitelist" / "no re-auditing" / "PURE, no sim" as separate tests) — a failing assertion in a smaller, precisely-named test is faster to diagnose than one covering four unrelated invariants.

decisions.title: P7 slice table restructured to eight slices (intent gate before pipeline; counterfactual solver and candidate generator get owning slices)
decisions.why_it_exists: The pre-P6 four/six-slice P7 table sequenced the REQ-091 intent gate AFTER the REQ-090 pipeline that must contain it, and gave no slice to the dm-0041 counterfactual solver or to candidate generation, which the creativity loop and pipeline both presuppose.
decisions.problem_it_solves: A pipeline assembled before its Sign-off/Intent phase exists ships a stub phase (no-placeholder violation); unowned deliverables are silently dropped work. S7.1 lifecycle, S7.2 counterfactual audit, S7.3 PDA, S7.4 concept+generator, S7.5 creativity loop, S7.6 intent gate, S7.7 pipeline composition, S7.8 report.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Trust that every pipeline phase the designer relies on is a finished, separately-tested engine, never a bolt-on.
decisions.misconception_created: None intended; guards the team misconception that composition slices may precede their parts.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Keeping the original order would force either a stub sign-off phase inside S7.4 (forbidden) or a pipeline rewrite at S7.5; folding the solver into the pipeline slice hides a distinct P4-family audit inside a gen/ slice.

decisions.title: Mechanic lifecycle registry lives in the design memory ledger behind a v1.0-to-v1.1 schema bump; nine stages defined; propose/apply split; Exhaustion and Retirement block selection
decisions.why_it_exists: REQ-082 needs a persistent 9-stage lifecycle registry. DesignMemory v1.0 already reserved mechanic_lifecycle_registry.mechanics as pinned-empty with the parse error naming P7 and a version bump as the unlock (dm-0040; P5 report Q3). The nine stages, absent from the repo, are defined as Introduction, Isolation, Development, Combination, Subversion, Mastery, Saturation, Exhaustion, Retirement — derived from the level-design chapter arc (introduce, apply, deepen, test mastery) plus exhaustion economics.
decisions.problem_it_solves: A second lifecycle store would fork the single source of truth for design memory; undefined stage names would be guessed at implementation time. Transitions are forward-only and evidence-gated via assessStage (advisory) / advanceStage (explicit validated act) — the CDRE propose/apply house pattern. isBlocked returns true for Exhaustion/Retirement; Retirement carries a prune-or-convert disposition.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Confidence that a mechanic is never overused past freshness: the campaign never asks players to re-chew an exhausted idea.
decisions.misconception_created: Stage names may read as psychological claims about players; they are design-usage classifications over observable evidence (usage counts, novelty trend, campaign mastery signal) only.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Standalone lifecycle JSON store: two sources of truth, drift. Auto-advancing stages from evidence without an explicit apply step: silent state changes, unauditable — same reason CDRE proposals are inert until ACCEPTED. Fewer/other stage names: the PRD fixes nine, Introduction first, Retirement ninth.

decisions.title: Counterfactual jump-relevance audit built on searchReachability with a jump-consumed forbidden predicate; zero modifications to Search.ts
decisions.why_it_exists: dm-0041 deferred the REQ-012 counterfactual solver to P7: a completion tape that never presses the jump is a necessary-but-not-sufficient kill proxy — a level where jumping is possible but irrelevant still passes it. P7 builds auditJumpRelevance(def, options) returning jump-necessary, jump-irrelevant (with a replayable no-jump witness), or inconclusive.
decisions.problem_it_solves: Proving the NEGATIVE (the level cannot be completed without the jump) requires exhaustive bounded search, not sampled play. SearchOptions.forbidden (built for S4.4 exploit filtration) taints any state whose jump lock has left its spawn phase, restricting the budgeted BFS to no-jump routes: goal reached means the jump is irrelevant (REQ-012 kill); frontier exhausted means the jump is necessary (pass); node cap hit is reported inconclusive.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Trust that the one jump MATTERS in every level the pipeline accepts — the axiom is enforced, not assumed.
decisions.misconception_created: None; the three-way verdict explicitly avoids the misconception that budget-bounded searches prove universals.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Stripping jump actions from the ACTIONS table inside Search.ts modifies verified P4 code for something the existing forbidden hook already expresses; sampling archetype runs without the jump cannot prove unreachability; making the kill proxy stricter on tapes alone cannot detect jump-irrelevant levels at all.

decisions.title: New src/gen/ subtree for design-time generation logic with one-way dependency, public-seam consumption, and isolation scans from the first module
decisions.why_it_exists: P7 code is design-time generation — not evaluation (eval/), not definition-time I/O (schema/), not interactive tooling (tools/, P8). Precedent: schema/ added at P2 (dm-0013), eval/ added at P4 (dm-0022). directory_structure.md is updated in the same change. GenProfile (genProfileSchemaVersion 1, strict-parsed, DEFAULT_GEN_PROFILE frozen) is a third calibration schema separate from ScoringProfile and CampaignProfile, extending the dm-0045 separation logic.
decisions.problem_it_solves: Placement drift and hidden coupling: nothing outside gen/ may import gen/ (one-way, like eval/); gen/ consumes evaluation only through public seams (evaluateLevel/judgeLevel per dm-0031, auditSolvability, probeEmergentFun, noveltyDivergence, auditJumpRelevance) — never gdos/ gate internals, campaign/ internals, systems/, or the engine directly; whitelist math; RNG only via threaded core/Rng state. Scans land at S7.1 with the first gen/ module — the P6 lesson (dm-0053 closed a scan gap that had been prose-only for four slices).
decisions.emotion_targeted: Confidence that the generator can be rewritten wholesale without touching the verified sim, evaluation, or campaign layers.
decisions.misconception_created: None intended.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Placing the pipeline in tools/ loses purity and node --test coverage; placing it inside eval/ muddies the evaluation isolation scans and the one-way eval rule; extending ScoringProfile or CampaignProfile with gen calibration couples three concerns and forces version bumps on verified schemas.

decisions.title: PDA is a deterministic data-fusion opportunity search over five consumed-by-reference signals, never raw geometry
decisions.why_it_exists: REQ-060 specifies a standalone discovery tool searching conceptual/structural/systemic opportunities. discoverOpportunities(inputs, profile) fuses: dead CoverageMatrix cells (conceptual), CDRE coverage-gap and recurring-rejection proposals, CampaignReport alerts and weak chapter health (systemic), emergent-fun kinetic anchors (structural, REQ-054 applied), and the lifecycle registry — emitting ranked typed DesignOpportunity records with rationale and source signals.
decisions.problem_it_solves: Where should the next level concept come from, answered from evidence the M2 stack already produces rather than intuition. All inputs are consumed by reference (dm-0045/dm-0046 pattern) and optional-degrading like CdreInputs; mandatory filters: findPriorArt blocks re-proposing REJECTED ideas (REQ-051), isBlocked excludes Exhausted/Retired mechanics.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Curiosity aimed where the design space is actually unexploited, giving players configurations the campaign has never shown them.
decisions.misconception_created: Opportunity rank may read as a guarantee of level quality; it is only a prioritized gap signal — the pipeline gates decide quality.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Generating raw geometry in the PDA conflates discovery with manufacturing (REQ-060 explicitly says NOT raw geometry); re-deriving coverage or campaign analytics inside the PDA duplicates verified P5/P6 computation; an unranked bag of gaps pushes prioritization back onto intuition.

decisions.title: LevelConcept carries the level-design-principle workflow answers including the intent sentence; parametric archetype templates generate schema-valid candidates deterministically
decisions.why_it_exists: The pipeline phase-1 record LevelConcept types the /level-design-principle design-workflow answers: archetype (closed twelve-value list), the one-jump decision, the intent sentence (REQ-091 subject), mechanics (lifecycle-checked), difficulty-vector target, emotional-arc phase, targetKgNode. generateCandidate(concept, seed, profile) maps a concept to a LevelDefinition via a parametric template per archetype family; mutateCandidate/combineCandidates are the creativity-loop operators.
decisions.problem_it_solves: Candidate generation with proofs: every emitted definition round-trips the P2 strict parser; same (concept, seed) yields a byte-identical candidate (threaded core/Rng, doubly-bounded loops, whitelist math); the intent sentence lives in the concept and the product provenance record, NOT in LevelDefinition — no LEVEL_SCHEMA_VERSION bump in P7; persistence into the shipped schema is P10 decision.
decisions.emotion_targeted: The full arc the archetypes encode — curiosity, tension, betrayal-with-fairness, mastery — selected per concept rather than defaulting to hidden-trap memory tests.
decisions.misconception_created: Generated fixtures may look like shipped content; they exist only inside tests — P7 authors no campaign content (M3/P10 gate).
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Grammar/wave-function-collapse/ML generation is nondeterministic or unverifiable under the project invariants; bumping LevelDefinition for the intent sentence forces P2 parser churn P7 does not need; free-form concept prose instead of typed fields would make the intent gate an NLP problem instead of a structural check.

decisions.title: The 8-phase manufacturing pipeline is a staged pure fold emitting an accepted product or a typed logged rejection; creativity loop doubly bounded; deletion means no product persisted, never no record
decisions.why_it_exists: REQ-090 names eight phases: Concept (lifecycle blocking), Structural Prototyping (generateCandidate + parse proof), Kinetic Simulation (P4 stack incl. auditJumpRelevance), AI Council Eval (evaluateLevel — the REQ-061 IDS regulation point), Targeted Revision (bounded re-generation against the failing gate), Optimization Layering (expert-route check), Sign-off/Intent (verifyIntent, REQ-091), Campaign Integration (LevelRecord-ready product with provenance). manufactureLevel(concept, corpus, seed, profile, options) is pure: same inputs, identical outcome.
decisions.problem_it_solves: The IRD P7 exit condition made testable: a fixture concept manufactures into a level passing P4 solvability and P5 gates, or is rejected at the correct phase with phase+reason+evidence and no silent drops. The REQ-081 creativity loop terminates by diminishing-returns epsilon or hard cap (both GenProfile); selection compares novelty divergence against the corpus (REQ-053 applied) plus profile-weighted gate scores.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Trust that every accepted level earned its place through the full gauntlet, and every rejection is explainable after the fact.
decisions.misconception_created: A deterministic pipeline may suggest one concept yields one possible level; the seed and profile are explicit inputs — variety is parameterized, not random.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: An event/callback pipeline reintroduces ordering nondeterminism; unbounded revision loops violate the halting discipline every search in this codebase honors; boolean rejections without provenance make REQ-091 deletion unauditable; assembling chapter-scoped LevelRecord.macroCriteria inside the pipeline exceeds a single level scope (chapter assembly stays with the caller/P10).

decisions.title: S7.4 template resolution: one gap-corridor skeleton, per-kind placement slots, all twelve mechanics templatable, parameter-level operators
decisions.why_it_exists: Resolves P7 plan open question 2 (template family size). The generator ships ONE parametric structural skeleton — the bordered gap corridor, the same shape the P4 audit fixtures prove jumpable — rather than twelve bespoke templates: approach floor, spiked pit sized by the concept executionPrecision, landing floor, goal. A frozen per-kind slot table places every EntityKind (spike = the pit itself; laser/movingHazard at the gap; spring/door on the landing; surfaces, plates, zones, movers on the approach; a plate+door pair is trigger-wired per dm-0015). GenParams (corridorLength, gapWidth, gapStart) is the operators working space: mutateCandidate jitters one clamped parameter, combineCandidates coin-flips each parameter from a parent, and every operator output is re-built and re-proven through the P2 strict parser.
decisions.problem_it_solves: Candidate generation with proofs and without fabricated variety: a generated level is a pure function of (concept, seed, profile); unbuildable concepts (mechanics exceeding the profiled corridor) are refused with typed reasons; the corridor envelope is auto-widened to fit a concept mechanic count before drawing. Every gameplay value (corridor range, air rows, gap ceiling, par tiers, entity dynamics) lives in GenProfile.generator + entityTuning.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Confidence that pipeline verification rests on proven-jumpable geometry; variety is parameterized, never random.
decisions.misconception_created: One skeleton may read as the final content vocabulary; it is the P7 verification substrate — P10 extends the template library (new skeletons) without restructuring this one.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Twelve bespoke templates: eleven more geometry proofs for zero additional pipeline-verification power. Def-level mutation (editing tiles): loses validity-by-construction and forces geometry repair. Placing movingPlatform over the gap: makes the pit crossable without the jump — the S7.2 counterfactual audit would kill every such candidate by design.

decisions.title: S7.5 creativity loop: monotone-best survivor fold, improvement = non-negative delta of best selection score, epsilon-or-hard-cap double bound
decisions.why_it_exists: Resolves P7 plan open question 3 (the diminishing-returns metric). evolveLevel realizes REQ-081 generate/vary/eval/compare-to-memory/select/improve as a deterministic fold: each generation breeds variationsPerGeneration variations of the current top-two survivors (mutate the best, or combine the top two into a hybrid with the profiled combineProbability), evaluates each through the real P5 seam (evaluateLevel), compares to memory via noveltyDivergence against a prior corpus (REQ-053 applied), scores by a profile-weighted composite of gate pass + mean gate score + clamped novelty, and keeps the top two. Because survivors carry forward, the best selection score is monotone non-decreasing, so IMPROVEMENT is the non-negative delta of that best between generations; the loop halts when improvement < diminishingReturnsEpsilon (converged) or at hardCapGenerations.
decisions.problem_it_solves: A convergence criterion that is both well-defined and testable. Monotonicity makes "improvement" unambiguous and gives epsilon a clean meaning: epsilon 0 is "never satisfied by convergence, run the full hard cap"; a large epsilon converges on the first non-breakthrough generation. Every stochastic step threads core/Rng; every candidate is the generator parse-proven output; evaluation is deterministic under a fixed seed, so the whole EvolutionResult is a pure function of (concept, corpus, seed, genProfile, evalOptions). An unbuildable seed concept fails honestly with the generator typed reason rather than silently returning nothing.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Confidence that iteration terminates and that each accepted level genuinely beat its predecessors on a stated composite — not luck, not an endless loop.
decisions.misconception_created: Monotone-best may read as "every generation improves"; it means the RETAINED best never regresses — a generation that breeds nothing better simply carries the incumbent and triggers convergence.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Improvement as best-of-generation delta (non-monotone) makes epsilon ambiguous and can oscillate, never converging. Population-mean improvement rewards flooding the pool with mediocre clones. Discarding the incumbent each generation loses the best level to RNG. A single hard-cap with no epsilon wastes evaluation budget on converged searches; a single epsilon with no cap risks non-termination on a pathological scorer.

decisions.title: S7.6 intent gate: rigor is a structural check (one sentence + word band + causal-connective grammar), not NLP
decisions.why_it_exists: REQ-091 denies compile to any layout that cannot state its lesson in one rigorous sentence. verifyIntent(concept, profile) judges the concept intentSentence structurally: exactly one sentence (exactly one terminal .!? ending the string), inside GenProfile.intent minWords..maxWords, and containing at least one causal connective from a closed grammar (because/so/when/only/unless/after/before/until/since/if/once/whenever/then). The connective is the marker that turns a LABEL into a LESSON with a reason. A denial is a typed IntentVerdict listing every failed reason (empty short-circuits) plus path-qualified findings.
decisions.problem_it_solves: A verifiable, deterministic gate under the zero-dependency + determinism invariants — NLP would be neither reproducible nor verifiable. The connective grammar is a structural constant (like the lifecycle stages); only the word band is calibration, so a two-profile test proves the length bar is external. deny-all-reasons (not short-circuit, except on empty) lets a caller see every problem at once. Deletion means the pipeline persists only the rejection record, never a product.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Trust that every shipped level has a single, legible reason to exist — the /level-design-principle brainstorming bar ("has one sentence explaining the solution").
decisions.misconception_created: The connective check may read as grammar policing; it is a rigor proxy — a lesson without a cause/condition word is almost always a description, not a decision. False positives (a rigorous sentence phrased without a listed connective) are acceptable: the author rephrases, and the connective set is easily extended.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: NLP/LLM sentence scoring: nondeterministic, unverifiable, external dependency. Length-only checking: a 20-word description passes while stating no decision (the plan warned against a vacuous length check). Requiring the literal word "jump": too brittle — a lesson may say "the leap" or "commit". Persisting the sentence into LevelDefinition to gate later: forces a schema bump P7 does not need (dm-0059) — the sentence stays in the concept and product provenance.

decisions.title: S7.7 pipeline Kinetic-Simulation gate: solvable + jump-necessary + softlock-free is the hard gate; the entity-exploit audit is recorded evidence, not a gate
decisions.why_it_exists: The REQ-090 pipeline phase 3 (Kinetic Simulation) must decide when a generated geometry is sound. Empirically (probed during S7.7), the entity-exploit audit (S4.4 auditExploit) reports hasExploit=true for EVERY well-formed one-jump pit — including the canonical hand-authored ONE_GAP fixture the S4.2 solvability suite treats as the reference solvable level — because a pit’s spikes are the fail-state (fall in = die), not a hazard the winning route is meant to touch, and the winning jump arc never enters the spike envelope. Treating exploit as a hard gate would therefore reject every one-jump pit the generator (or a human) can produce.
decisions.problem_it_solves: A correct, non-self-defeating soundness gate. Phase 3 hard-gates on solvable (S4.2) AND jump-necessary (S7.2 counterfactual, the REQ-012 check) AND softlock-free (S4.3). Jump-necessity is precisely the SUFFICIENT load-bearing-challenge check that dm-0041 said the kill/exploit proxies could not provide: it proves the one jump is required, which is the real question the exploit heuristic was approximating for one-jump levels. The exploit verdict (bypassedHazardIds) is still RUN and recorded in the product/rejection provenance as evidence, so a genuine entity-hazard skip on a future multi-hazard template remains visible — it is simply not the phase-3 kill switch for geometry-challenge levels.
decisions.emotion_targeted: Trust that the pipeline accepts exactly the levels whose one jump genuinely matters, and rejects the rest, without a heuristic that misfires on the game’s defining shape.
decisions.misconception_created: Recording-but-not-gating exploit may read as ignoring it; on a template whose challenge is entity hazards (not a geometry gap) a bypass would still surface in provenance for a caller/P10 to act on — and the pipeline could add a template-aware exploit gate when such templates exist.
decisions.why_alternatives_rejected: Hard-gating on exploit: rejects every one-jump pit, including ONE_GAP (proven). Tuning auditExploit margins to hide the pit signal: hides a real signal and is fragile. Dropping the counterfactual audit and trusting exploit: exactly the necessary-but-insufficient proxy dm-0041 rejected. Removing the spikes to dodge the flag: an empty pit traps the player (softlock) or requires out-of-bounds death the engine does not model.

mechanic_lifecycle_registry.notes: Populated from Phase 3 onward. Tracks each mechanic across the 9-stage spectrum (Introduction→Reinforcement→Expansion→Combination→Subversion→Mastery→Dormancy→Reintroduction→Retirement).
